name: Scratch: Creative Coding
overview: Scratch is the world's most successful programming environment for kids. Visual blocks replace syntax, but every core concept — loops, conditionals, variables, events — is real.
Over 36 lessons, students build 8–10 finished projects: animations, mini-games, interactive stories. By the end of the year, they're ready for text-based code.
Lessons run 60 minutes. Each one ends with a small deliverable parents can see at home — which drives renewals.

modules.title: Module 3: First Game
modules.summary: Maze game with scoring, lives, and win condition.

modules.title: Module 6: Arcade Classics
modules.summary: Pong, Catch the Apple, Brick Breaker.

modules.title: Module 8: Capstone
modules.summary: Students design and ship their own original game.

steps.activity: Teacher demo: drawing the maze backdrop
Students build their own maze (worksheet step-by-step)
Add the player sprite + keyboard movement
Show & tell: each student demos their maze

projects: A 2D maze game with multiple levels
An interactive story with branching choices
prerequisites: None — this is a beginner course. Basic reading required.
